question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractResultRepository extends CrudRepository (which exposes deleteById(Long id)) to attempt deletion of a specific ContractResult by its consensusTimestamp ID through any exposed API endpoint, selectively removing a single record from history without triggering the retention policy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the processContractStateChange() path where hook storage changes are routed to evmHookStorageHandler instead of the normal state-change path, causing state changes for a hook execution to be recorded under a different transaction context than the one visible in ContractResult history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCall transaction with a topLevelAction at callDepth=0 that reports a gasUsed value of zero, causing calculateGasConsumed() to set gasConsumed to only the intrinsic gas cost, making the transaction appear to have consumed far less gas than its neighbors and distorting the gas-ordered history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() query targets the ContractResult JPA entity (not the underlying contract_result table directly) to bypass any database-level row security policies that might protect certain rows, allowing deletion of records that should be protected?
